title: Edge Hill Student Daniel Heads to Washington DC for Global AI Computing Final

image.alt: Daniel reviewing his project notebooks before leaving for Washington DC

summary.heading: The Summary
rows: Washington DC, USA, for a two-week programme
Global AI Computing Competition with teams from universities worldwide
Applied AI system developed in his Research and Development Project

abstract: Edge Hill student Daniel will represent his course in Washington DC after his third-year Research and Development Project was selected for a two-week global AI computing competition.
intro: What started as a final-year challenge has quickly become an international opportunity. Daniel's Research and Development Project has earned him a place in a global AI computing competition, where he will spend two weeks in Washington DC presenting, testing, and refining his work alongside student teams from across the world.

image.alt: Student developer preparing AI model outputs on a laptop

paragraphs: Daniel's project builds an AI workflow that balances performance, explainability, and practical deployment — a combination judges said made it stand out. After a year of supervisor feedback, user testing, and benchmarking, it grew from a coursework submission into a serious piece of technical work with real-world applications in sectors where transparent AI decisions matter.

image.alt: Delegates networking at an international computing event

paragraphs: The Washington DC programme combines live judging rounds, technical mentoring, and collaboration with finalists from across the world. Daniel will present his architecture, defend his design choices, and run live demonstrations. For students watching his progress, it is a clear message that ambitious project work can open doors far beyond the classroom.
